name: Bacon Swiss Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Assemble the rotor slicer/shredder using a coarse shredder cone (No 2).
2. Turn to speed 4 and shred the potatoes and onion separately. Set aside.
3. Switch to a fine shredder cone (No 1) and shred the cheese. Set aside.
4. Fry the bacon in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat until very crisp. Remove from the pan and crumble, leaving out all but 1/4 cup of bacon fat. Add the onion and sauté for 3 minutes. Set aside.
5. In a medium saucepan, combine 2 cups shredded cheese, eggs, heavy cream, nutmeg, salt, pepper, and garlic. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the cheese melts. Do not boil. Add the potatoes and onion, mixing well.
6. Pour half of the cheese/potato mixture into a greased 9x9x1-1/2-inch pan. Sprinkle half of the bacon on top. Repeat with the remaining cheese/potato and bacon. Top with the remaining cheese.
7. Bake at 400°F for 45 to 50 minutes, or until bubbly and golden brown.
8. Serve immediately.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
